root_cause: Application is calling Service 'payments' on port 8443, but the Service only exposes port 443
evidence: Recent workload failures reference Service 'payments' on port 8443, but the Service publishes port(s) 443
Port mismatch symptoms were observed 3 time(s) within the last 20 minutes
Service 'payments' has ready endpoints, so the failure is not explained by empty backends
Requested port 8443 matches Service targetPort, suggesting clients are using the backend port instead of the Service port
Workload configuration explicitly references the mismatched Service port

causes.message: Workload is attempting to reach Service 'payments' on port 8443
Service 'payments' exposes port(s) 443, but clients are using port 8443
The Service name resolves, but traffic is sent to a port that the Service does not publish

service:payments: Ready endpoints exist for the Service
pod:checkout-api-6c74b7d879-rm4gv: Error: failed to connect to https://payments.default.svc.cluster.local:8443/authorize: dial tcp 10.96.18.54:8443: connect: connection refused

likely_causes: The application is hardcoded to use the backend targetPort or containerPort instead of Service.spec.ports[*].port
A Helm value or application setting still points to an old Service port after the Service was changed
Client-side dependency configuration drifted from the Kubernetes Service definition
suggested_checks: Compare the application's configured dependency URL/port with Service.spec.ports[*].port rather than targetPort
